title: RuleSentry v3 Extension
description: Versioned extension manifest that can introduce additional categories, rules, evaluations, or policies under a namespace. Extensions are distributed artifacts that require authorship metadata for trust, licensing, and support.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for this document (optional but recommended).

type.description: Top-level entity type discriminator. Always 'extension' for extension manifests.

name.description: Short namespace identifier for this extension (lowercase, no spaces). Used as prefix for extension-specific entities (e.g., 'hipaa', 'pci', 'aws').

caption.description: Human-friendly display name for this extension.

description.description: Detailed description of what this extension provides and why it exists.

uid.description: Globally unique extension identifier. Must be reserved via the RuleSentry extension registry to prevent collisions. Range 1-999 reserved for official extensions, 1000+ for community/private extensions, 9000-9999 for private/internal use.

namespace.description: DEPRECATED: Use 'name' instead. Logical namespace for this extension (e.g., customer.acme, rulesentry.core).
namespace.$comment: Will be removed in the next major schema version. Use 'name' field instead.

author.description: Name of the person or organization that created this extension.

author_email.description: Contact email for the extension author (for support, bug reports, etc.).

author_url.description: URL to the author's website or organization page.

license.description: SPDX license identifier or custom license name. Critical for legal compliance when distributing extensions.

repository.description: URL to the source code repository (e.g., GitHub, GitLab).

homepage.description: URL to the extension's homepage or documentation site.

bugs_url.description: URL to report bugs or issues (e.g., GitHub issues).

extends.description: Which core types this extension extends (e.g., category, rule, evaluation, policy).

profiles.description: Profile identifiers that this extension provides or implements. Extensions can define new profiles (e.g., a HIPAA extension provides the 'hipaa' profile) and/or implement existing profiles. Rules within this extension typically use these profiles for cross-cutting metadata. Examples: ['hipaa', 'healthcare'], ['pci-dss', 'financial'], ['aws', 'cloud'].

dependencies.description: Extension dependencies. Keys are extension names, values are semver ranges.

min_engine_version.description: Minimum RuleSentry engine version required to use this extension.

signature.description: Digital signature for extension verification. Ensures integrity and authenticity.

public_key.description: Base64-encoded public key for signature verification.

signature.description: Base64-encoded signature of the extension content hash.

examples.name: hipaa
examples.caption: HIPAA Healthcare Extension
examples.description: Healthcare-specific rules and categories for HIPAA compliance, including PHI classification and patient consent tracking.
examples.author: Acme Healthcare Inc
